metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1025
metadata.comment: Rationale: Authors use a sh-RNA to knockdown CNA gamma in rat primary hippocampal neurons and monitor dynamics of the synaptic vesicle cycle of excitatory synapses using Vglut1-pHluorin. Analysis of the Vglut1-pHluorin responses after 10Hz 100 action potential stimulation revealed a mild, non significant effect of CNA gamma knockdown in synaptic vesicle exocytosis. Analysis of endocytosis kinetics, however, revealed a significant decrease of the time constant of fluorescence recovery, tau, which was rescued by over expression of a shRNA resistant CNA gamma vector. Analysis of the reacidification kinetics discarded an impairment of vesicle reacidification and supported the role of CNA gamma in synaptic vesicle endocytosis. This role seems specific of the gamma isoform and not beta or alpha, since over expression of these did not rescue the endocytosis phenotype, and over expression of the gamma isoform carrying a point mutation in the catalytic domain which inactivates calcineurin could also not rescue the phenotype.. Experimental description: "Rat hippocampal neurons were cotransfected with a Ppp3cc-specific shRNA and the vGlut1- pHluorin construct." "Oligonucleotides of the rat Ppp3cc cDNA target sequence (CGGCTAACTTTGA- AGGAAGTA, Invitrogen) were annealed and ligated into pSUPER"
